name: Python / SciPy
description: The general-purpose scientific computing stack that supports data analysis, plotting, optimisation, and lightweight modelling across quantum research and engineering.
whyUseIt: Even in quantum work, a huge amount of useful engineering happens outside specialised SDKs. Python and SciPy are where raw experiment data, calibration fits, and trade-study logic often live.
fieldPosition: Cross-cutting analysis, visualisation, and notebook-based experimentation
engineerWorkflow: Use notebooks or scripts to clean data, fit models, plot results, and automate supporting analysis around quantum experiments or simulations.
managerExpects: Readable code, documented assumptions, and outputs that can be rerun when the experiment changes.
demoDescription: Take measurement or simulation results from a quantum experiment and turn them into a small analysis notebook with clear plots and interpretation.
beginnerMistakes: Using notebooks as scratchpads with no reproducible structure
Producing polished plots without checking whether the underlying model makes sense
